claim: Tier 7.7v-InputDiversity confirms the input redundancy hypothesis: replacing the 7-element EMA trace bank (all convolving the same scalar x) with causal multi-lag temporal differencing at lags [1,2,4,8,16,32,64,128] restores state dimensionality. Diff-embedding PR=4.76 vs orthogonal baseline PR=2.01 (2.37x improvement) across Mackey-Glass/Lorenz/repaired-NARMA10 at 8000 steps, seeds 42/43/44. This identifies the EMA trace bank as the primary low-rank collapse bottleneck.
caveat: Input diversity hypothesis test only. Not a promoted mechanism, not a baseline freeze, not public usefulness proof, and not hardware/native transfer. Expanded confirmation at longer lengths needed before promotion.
